_comment: Two lone Infantry regiments, side by side, each given the same quarter-turn WALK-gait move order. The left one is disciplined (default): it holds during the pre-march reform, centre-pivoting gradually onto its new heading before stepping off as one formed body. The right one is undisciplined: it snaps its facing onto the destination immediately and walks there directly, re-forming as it arrives rather than turning as a body.
